name: Colectomy vascular anatomy
review_note: Vascular tree is side-dependent; confirm the named pedicles and ureter relationship for the specific colectomy side against your atlas.

intake.why: The vascular tree differs by side

figure_scopes.overview: Colon segment, mesocolon, and the side-appropriate vascular tree
figure_scopes.storyboard: Orientation -> vascular pedicle/marginal artery -> safe dissection plane (ureter protected) -> resection concept
figure_scopes.closeup: Named vascular pedicle with the ureter kept posterior to the mesocolic plane

laterality: Side-dependent (right vs left/sigmoid); the ureter is retroperitoneal and posterior to the mesocolic dissection plane
must_have: Colon segment of interest, mesocolon, named feeding vessels, marginal artery, and relevant ureter/gonadal vessel safety landmarks.
Superior mesenteric artery/vein or inferior mesenteric artery/vein context appropriate to the colectomy side.
Lymphovascular pedicle or mesenteric window only if requested.
spatial_rules: Right colectomy anatomy should use ileocolic/right colic/middle colic relationships as appropriate.
Left/sigmoid colectomy anatomy should use inferior mesenteric artery, left colic, sigmoid branches, and superior rectal artery relationships.
Ureter should remain retroperitoneal and posterior to the mesocolic dissection plane.

forbidden_errors.error: Ureter placed inside the mesocolon or vessel pedicle.
Right and left colectomy vascular trees mixed in one unlabeled anatomy map.
An invented continuous vessel branch that contradicts the selected colectomy side.

safety_concept: Match the vascular tree to the colectomy side and keep the ureter protected, posterior to the mesocolic plane.
phases: Named vascular pedicle and marginal artery map.
Safe dissection plane with the ureter/retroperitoneal landmark protected.
verify: Vascular tree matches the selected colectomy side.
Ureter and retroperitoneal landmarks are protected and not inside the pedicle.
Marginal artery/arcade continuity is anatomically plausible.
